items.check: Is the main idea stated in the first sentence?
Is it within the target length (~100 words)?
Did you use at least 3 new vocabulary words from this phase?
Are verb tenses consistent throughout?
Did you check articles (a/an/the) on every noun?
Are plural nouns correct (no missing -s)?
Is the tone neutral/objective (not opinionated)?
Is there a clear, specific subject line?
Does the opening match the relationship (formal vs internal)?
Is the request or purpose stated in the first paragraph?
Did you avoid contractions if this is a formal email?
Subject-verb agreement checked?
Is there a clear closing and call to action?
Did you re-read for prepositions (in/on/at/for)?
Did you use 'shall' or 'must' for every obligation?
Did you use 'may' only for rights/discretion?
Are the parties clearly named and defined?
Is any condition introduced with 'provided that' / 'subject to'?
Did you avoid ambiguous words (reasonable, promptly) without definition?
Is each sentence about one obligation or right only?
Did you compare it to a similar clause in the library?
